title: What does France's assisted dying bill contain, as MPs prepare to cast their final vote?
subhead: As it reaches the end of its parliamentary journey with Wednesday's vote in the  Assemblée Nationale, the law would establish the right to assisted suicide or euthanasia for patients experiencing constant suffering from serious and incurable illnesses, under strictly regulated conditions.

title: René Obermann, chairman of Airbus: 'We must put an end to industrial nationalism in defense'

US targets ICC in new offensive
subhead: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio reopened a diplomatic campaign on Monday aimed at dismantling the ICC by combining sanctions, putting pressure on member states and seeking allies among major powers hostile to the institution.

title: Ukraine expands attacks in Sea of Azov, a crucial maritime corridor for Russia
subhead: After choking off Crimea, which Moscow has occupied since 2014, Ukrainian drones are waging a relentless naval campaign against Russia's so-called 'shadow fleet,' which has been accused of evading sanctions. This has brought the traffic of these vessels on the Sea of Azov to a near halt.

title: Spain picks apart Mbappé's powerless France in World Cup semi-final
subhead: French captain Kylian Mbappé and his teammates fell to a Spanish side that outclassed them in every aspect of the game.

title: Judge of 2016 Nice terror attack trial reflects on 'abominable' case, 10 years after
subhead: Laurent Raviot presided over the Paris special criminal court that held the trial of the attack in Nice that killed 86 people on July 14, 2016. On the 10th anniversary of the attack, he granted Le Monde his first interview, speaking with unusual candor.

title: 'You're expected to perform, but no one wants to know how you do it': Cocaine, the toxic fuel of young professionals
subhead: Cocaine at work (1/3). Among young professionals, the illegal and highly addictive drug is used in a relentless pursuit of performance, often shrouded in taboo and without questioning of working conditions.

title: Macron and Zelensky, a special relationship marked by misunderstandings
subhead: Ukrainian soldiers will join France's annual Bastille Day military parade in Paris on Tuesday, July 14. French President Emmanuel Macron and his Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelensky will stand together in a sign of Paris's support for Kyiv, but their relationship has not always been so straightforward.
